developer_response.body: Hi Princessdydy, thank you so much for your support! Regarding the mutation issues, here's a pro tip: You don’t actually need to align it 100% perfectly to succeed! If the patient's hand is moving too much, try grabbing their hand first to stabilize it before installing the part. It makes the process much easier! Good luck!

this game is really fun and cool, i’m really looking forward to the next update. i hope there will be more outfit options for the characters. this is a small suggestion from me.

Trust me — I’ve studied for years — I’ll take good care of you… mostly. Unless I suddenly feel like having fun, in which case things might not end so well! Crazy Doctor: Treat or Trick puts you in the role of a doctor with a mix of complex equipment and completely delirious tools to “cure” your equally crazy patients.

The best part? The patients don’t seem to mind being guinea pigs, which makes for endless hours of bizarre experiments, hilarious situations, and unpredictable outcomes. Between serious medical gear and over-the-top options, the game strikes a fun balance of simulation and chaos.

A quirky, entertaining, and highly replayable VR experience — trust the science (or at least, this version of it).
search a good game it is so fun i feel like a real doctor
such a fun game highly recomed

Crazy Doctor keeps surprising me even after quite a few hours in. The idea is simple but really addictive: you take on the role of a doctor, doing your best to cure patients of all sorts of strange ailments.

At the start, you’ve only got the most basic tools and very little to work with. That means you have to focus on the essentials: taking care of your patients, keeping them happy, and trying not to mess anything up. As you succeed, you start earning more money and building up your reputation. That’s when the game really opens up, because you can unlock more advanced diagnostic tools and buy proper medical equipment. Suddenly, you’re running a much more professional-looking practice… or at least, trying to!

I like how the game balances between being lighthearted and challenging. You never know what kind of patient is going to walk through the door next, and figuring out their condition feels rewarding. But you also have to be careful, without proper training (and with some questionable methods), you can get some extremely shocking treatment results...

Overall, Crazy Doctor is a quirky, entertaining VR experience, with that “just one more patient” kind of pull that keeps you coming back. If you enjoy slightly offbeat VR titles that mix fun with a bit of chaos, this one is definitely worth checking out.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for your feedback! We're thrilled you're enjoying Crazy Doctor, and we'll keep adding more fun content and new features—stay tuned!

Play as a doctor in the most crazy and wacky ways. Cure patients with unique and non-traditional procedures. Or cause chaos by using unethical, immoral, and illegal procedures to the most annoying patients. But if you want to get paid to unlock all the treatment options in the medicine cabinet, be nice, do the right thing, and earn positive reviews. After that, go rogue! lol Check out my breaking news YT video about exposing a crazy doctor @misschiefvr.

developer_response.body:
Wow, we just watched your "Crazy Doctor News" video and it's absolutely brilliant!
We love seeing players get this creative with our game. It's awesome how you found so many hilarious and unique ways to play within the different procedures.  Thanks for making such fantastic content and for sharing it with your followers! We can't wait to see what other crazy videos you come up with next!

developer_response.body:
Hi smoky.bear4202,

Latest Update Response: Since receiving your previous feedback, our team has been working hard to address the issues and requests you mentioned. We’re excited to let you know that the newest update has just been released!

In this version, the problem with the X-ray glasses not functioning has been fixed. Regarding your request for more healing support, after careful research and discussion, we wanted to maintain the sense of exploration in the game while still offering additional help. To achieve this, we’ve added a feature that allows disease images on the PAD to be enlarged. With this enhancement, players can see the symptoms more clearly, which we hope will improve your overall gameplay experience.

We’d love for you to try out the updated version and see the improvements for yourself!

same two elementary all the time

developer_response.body:
Dear S8oD.Lacey,

Thank you so much for taking the time to review Crazy Doctor. We read your feedback, "same two elementary all the time," and we want to start by offering our deepest apologies. You are absolutely right, and we completely understand how frustrating it must have been to encounter that repetitive bug.

Your specific report was instrumental in helping us quickly identify and squash this critical issue. We have since released an update that ensures the disease variety is now working exactly as intended.

We truly appreciate your patience and valuable support of our game. As developers, seeing this kind of honest feedback helps us deliver a better experience for everyone.

We humbly ask that you update the game and give Crazy Doctor one more chance. We are confident you will now find the diverse and fun gameplay you originally expected.

If you'd like to reach us directly, get instant news, or share more ideas, please join our friendly Discord community here: discord.gg/gqwss4SFAp

Thank you again for your understanding.

The Crazy Doctor Development Team
